metadata.title: Guidelines on the quality, safety and efficacy of plasmid DNA vaccines
metadata.document_version: WHO Technical Report Series, No. 1028, Annex 2 (71st report of WHO Expert Committee on Biological Standardization) — replaces Annex 1 of WHO TRS No. 941 (2007)
metadata.note: No prior extraction found in output-v2/v3/v4. Full extraction performed. Document covers quality (Part A), nonclinical (Part B), clinical (Part C), and NRA guidance (Part D) for plasmid DNA vaccines intended for prevention of infectious diseases in humans.

rules.rule_text: Plasmid DNA vaccines for clinical trials must be prepared under GMP conditions suitable for the stage of clinical development; full compliance may not be possible in initial or early development when manufacturing and control procedures remain in development and may not yet be validated.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.2 — General manufacturing guidelines
rules.verbatim_quote: DNA vaccines for use in clinical trials should also be prepared under GMP conditions suitable for the stage of clinical development – that is, full compliance may not be possible in initial or early development when manufacturing and control procedures remain in development and may not yet be validated.
rules.exceptions: In early development, procedures shared with other DNA vaccines already validated at higher phases may be used.
cross_references: WHO GMP for pharmaceutical products TRS 986 Annex 2
WHO GMP for biological products TRS 999 Annex 2

rules.rule_text: Cleaning validation must be performed even in early development for multi-use facilities or contract manufacturing organizations, including verification with assays sensitive enough to detect residual full-length plasmid DNA from prior lots.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.2 — General manufacturing guidelines
rules.verbatim_quote: Cleaning validation would be expected even in early development for such multi-use facilities, even though such validation would normally occur later... One novel aspect particular to DNA vaccines is that cleaning procedures need to be verified with an assay sensitive enough to detect not only microorganisms and other biological materials that may be residual from prior manufacture, but also residual full-length plasmid DNA from prior lots of products made using the same equipment and facility.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: The nucleotide sequence of the entire plasmid DNA vaccine must be provided with appropriate annotation indicating important elements including promoters/enhancers, termination sequences, drug-resistance or selection markers, and the bacterial origin of replication.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.2.1.2 — Source, history and generation of the host cell and plasmid
rules.verbatim_quote: The nucleotide sequence of the entire plasmid DNA vaccine should be provided together with appropriate annotation indicating the important elements, such as the promoters/enhancers, termination sequences, drug-resistance or other marker for selection in bacteria, and bacterial origin of replication.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: A DNA sequence homology check of the plasmid against international databases (e.g., NCBI, NIH) must be performed to investigate the presence of unintended sequences of biological significance, such as those encoding cellular growth factors, known immunogens, or viral sequences.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.2.1.2 — Source, history and generation of the host cell and plasmid
rules.verbatim_quote: As part of characterization, a DNA sequence homology check of the plasmid against international databases (for example, the National Center for Biotechnology Information, National Institutes of Health, the USA and/or other international nucleotide databases) should be performed to investigate the presence of unintended sequences of biological significance, such as those encoding cellular growth factors, other known immunogens or viral sequences.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: The identity of the plasmid after transformation into the bacterial cell to be used for production must be confirmed, preferably by sequencing. Freedom from cross-contaminating plasmids must be controlled and verified.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.2.1.2 — Source, history and generation of the host cell and plasmid
rules.verbatim_quote: The identity of the plasmid after transformation into the bacterial cell to be used for production should be confirmed. While sequencing is preferred, representative restriction enzyme maps may also be useful. The candidate vaccine selected to advance in clinical development and for marketing authorization should be demonstrated to be genetically stable. Freedom from cross-contaminating plasmids should be controlled and verified.
rules.exceptions: Restriction enzyme maps may be used in place of sequencing.

rules.rule_text: Plasmid DNA vaccine production must be based ideally on a cell bank system involving a master cell bank (MCB) and a working cell bank (WCB); initiating production from a well-characterized WCB is expected for commercial manufacturing.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.2.1.3 — Cell bank system, characterization and testing
rules.verbatim_quote: The production of a plasmid DNA vaccine should be based ideally on a cell bank system involving a master cell bank (MCB) and a working cell bank (WCB)... Initiating production from a well-characterized WCB is expected for commercial manufacturing.
rules.exceptions: For early-stage clinical trials, it may be appropriate to use the MCB to initiate production, though manufacturers are expected to prepare a WCB for later clinical studies.

rules.rule_text: The preparation of MCB and WCB must be conducted according to GMP with appropriate precautions to prevent contamination. Evidence for viability under storage and recovery conditions must be provided by the time of marketing authorization application.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.2.1.3 — Cell bank system, characterization and testing
rules.verbatim_quote: The preparation of the MCB and WCB should be conducted according to GMP with appropriate precautions taken to prevent contamination. Information should be provided on the origin and storage conditions of the cell banks. Evidence for the viability of the MCB and WCB under storage and recovery conditions should also be provided by the time of application for marketing authorization.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: The genetic stability of the plasmid must be confirmed by characterizing its size and complete nucleotide sequence throughout the fermentation process.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.2.1.3 — Cell bank system, characterization and testing
rules.verbatim_quote: The genetic stability of the plasmid should be confirmed by characterizing its size and complete nucleotide sequence throughout the fermentation process.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Process validation must demonstrate that the manufacturing process yields a product consistently meeting predefined quality attributes, including demonstrable and consistent clearance of process and product-related contaminants to levels acceptable for the intended use in humans.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.2.2 — Process development and in-process control
rules.verbatim_quote: Validation of the manufacturing process should be shown to yield a product consistently meeting the predefined quality attributes, including demonstration of reproducible and consistent clearance of process and product-related contaminants to levels acceptable for the intended use in humans.
rules.exceptions: Process validation is not generally required for a product used in early-stage clinical trials, but critical steps (aseptic processing, sterility, cleaning validation) must be validated or carefully controlled prior to manufacture of clinical materials.

rules.rule_text: Specifications for the bulk purified plasmid must include, at a minimum, an assessment of identity, purity, physical state and quantity of the plasmid, along with endotoxin content, and sterility or bioburden. A justification of the specifications must be provided.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.4 — Control of bulk purified plasmid
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that the specifications for the bulk purified plasmid include, at a minimum, an assessment of the identity, purity, physical state and quantity of the plasmid, along with the endotoxin content, and the sterility or bioburden of the bulk. A justification of the specifications should be provided.
rules.exceptions: In early development, specifications may be limited and have somewhat wide acceptance criteria.

rules.rule_text: The identity of each bulk purified plasmid batch must be confirmed by an appropriate method such as PCR analysis, sequencing, restriction enzyme analysis, or in vitro expression (mRNA or protein) of the gene insert, accompanied by confirmation of the identity of the expressed antigen.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.4.1 — Identity
rules.verbatim_quote: The identity of each bulk purified plasmid batch should be confirmed by an appropriate method such as PCR analysis, sequencing, restriction enzyme analysis or in vitro expression (mRNA or protein) of the gene insert of the plasmid accompanied by confirmation of the identity of the expressed antigen.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: For bulk plasmid purity, the degree of contamination with host cell chromosomal DNA, RNA, and proteins must be evaluated and limits established and acceptance criteria specified. A maximum allowable limit for all impurities must be established and justified.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.4.2 — Purity
rules.verbatim_quote: The degree of contamination with host cell chromosomal DNA, RNA and proteins should be evaluated and limits established, and acceptance criteria established and specified... A maximum allowable limit should be established and justified.
rules.exceptions: Measuring residual levels of process- or product-related impurities may be discontinued after suitable processes for their removal have been adequately validated.

rules.rule_text: The proportion of supercoiled plasmid in the bulk purified plasmid must be determined and specifications set. Quantification of plasmid amount is based on absorbance at 260 nm.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.4.3 — Physical state and quantification of plasmid
rules.verbatim_quote: The proportion of supercoiled plasmid should be determined and specifications set. Quantification of the plasmid amount is usually based upon absorbance at 260 nm.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Safety tests for bulk purified plasmid must include tests for endotoxins, bacterial and fungal sterility (or bioburden including quantity, identification and freedom from specified unwanted organisms). A pyrogenicity test may be performed if required by the NRA.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.4.4 — Safety
rules.verbatim_quote: Relevant safety tests should be described and may include tests for: (a) endotoxins; (b) bacterial and fungal sterility (including demonstration of lack of bactericidal or fungicidal activity of the test article); or (c) bioburden (including quantity, identification and freedom from specified unwanted organisms). Although a test for pyrogenicity may be performed if required by the NRA, animal testing should be avoided whenever alternative satisfactory testing is accepted.
rules.exceptions: Animal testing should be avoided whenever alternative satisfactory testing is accepted. The 3Rs concept (Replace Reduce Refine) should be applied.

rules.rule_text: The general safety (innocuity) test must NOT be required or requested for plasmid DNA vaccines, following the WHO Expert Committee on Biological Standardization decision in 2018 to discontinue this test from routine lot release testing for all vaccines.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.4.4 — Safety
rules.verbatim_quote: manufacturers and regulators should take note of the decision of the WHO Expert Committee on Biological Standardization in 2018 to discontinue the inclusion of the general safety (innocuity) test in routine lot release testing requirements for all vaccines in WHO Recommendations, Guidelines and other guidance documents for biological products (82). This test should therefore not be required or requested.
rules.exceptions: None.

rules.rule_text: An in-house reference preparation must be established for use in assay standardization. A suitable batch that has been clinically evaluated should be fully characterized and retained for use as a chemical and biological reference material. A plan for replacing the initial reference material upon exhaustion must be agreed with the NRA.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.5 — Reference materials
rules.verbatim_quote: An in-house reference preparation should be established for use in assay standardization... A suitable batch (that is, one that has been clinically evaluated) should be fully characterized in terms of its chemical composition, purity, biological activity and complete sequence, and retained for use as a chemical and biological reference material. A plan for replacing the initial reference material upon exhaustion should be agreed with the NRA.
rules.exceptions: In early development, an engineering run batch may be used until a suitable clinical trial lot has been identified.

rules.rule_text: Stability assessment of bulk purified plasmid must be in compliance with WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines and must include long-term, real-time stability studies under actual conditions to support the shelf-life.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.3.6 — Stability
rules.verbatim_quote: The stability assessment should be in compliance with the WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines (83)... Stability data to support the shelf-life of the bulk and any future extension of it should be derived from long-term, real-time stability studies under actual conditions.
rules.exceptions: Limited stability information (e.g., three months real-time stability) may be acceptable during initial clinical development, to be agreed with the NRA.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines TRS 962 Annex 3

rules.rule_text: The final composition of the vaccine must be described. If the vaccine must be delivered by a specific method or device for established safety and efficacy, this must also be described.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.4.1 — Composition
rules.verbatim_quote: The final composition of the vaccine should be described. If it is required for established safety and efficacy that the vaccine needs to be delivered by a specific method or device then this should also be described.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: A flowchart must be provided illustrating all manufacturing steps from the bulk purified plasmid to the final formulated vaccine, including all unit operations, identification of materials, in-process and quality control tests, with a narrative description of each process step.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.4.2 — Manufacture
rules.verbatim_quote: A flowchart should be provided illustrating the manufacturing steps from the bulk purified plasmid to the final formulated vaccine. This flowchart should include all steps (that is, unit operations), identification of materials and in-process and quality control tests... A narrative description of each process step depicted in the flowchart should be provided.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Specifications for the final formulated vaccine must include identity, purity, physical state and quantity of the plasmid, potency, endotoxin content, and sterility. A justification of the specifications must be provided.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.4.4 — Control of final formulated vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that the specifications include an assessment of the identity, purity, physical state and quantity of the plasmid, any other relevant quality parameters, potency, endotoxin content and sterility. A justification of the specifications should be provided.
rules.exceptions: In early development, specifications may be limited with wide acceptance criteria.

rules.rule_text: Identity of each lot of vaccine must be confirmed by appropriate tests including restriction enzyme mapping, sequencing, and/or PCR.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.4.4.1 — Identity
rules.verbatim_quote: Each lot of vaccine should be subjected to an appropriate selection of the tests used to confirm the identity of the final product plasmid. Depending on the scope of the identification tests, confirmation of identity by restriction enzyme mapping, sequencing and/or PCR should be considered.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: The purity of each lot of vaccine must be determined and shown to be within specified limits. The form of the final product plasmid(s) must be confirmed (e.g., by gel electrophoresis) that the vaccine has not degraded. Container-closure system compatibility, leachables and extractables must be assessed.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.4.4.2 — Purity
rules.verbatim_quote: The purity of each lot of vaccine should be determined and shown to be within specified limits. The form of the final product plasmid(s) should be confirmed – for example, by conducting gel electrophoresis or other method to demonstrate that the vaccine has not degraded. Container-closure system compatibility, leachables and extractables should be assessed and discussed.
rules.exceptions: In multi-product facilities, freedom from contamination must be demonstrated to established limits or below detection.

rules.rule_text: DNA vaccines are dosed based on the quantity of the plasmid by weight, generally established by absorbance at 260 nm. Percentage of supercoiled plasmid must be assessed as an important quality parameter.
rules.source_section: Part A, Sections A.4.4.3 and A.4.4.4 — Content/strength/quantity and Other quality parameters
rules.verbatim_quote: DNA vaccines are dosed based on the quantity of the plasmid by weight. Generally, this is established by absorbance at 260 nm... Another important quality parameter is the percentage of the overall amount of plasmid that is supercoiled.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Potency of each lot of vaccine must be determined using a suitably quantitative and validated assay with an appropriate in-house reference preparation. Ideally a potency assay ensures consistency of lots with established clinical performance; often an in vitro expression system is used.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.4.4.5 — Potency
rules.verbatim_quote: The potency of each lot of the vaccine should be determined using a suitably quantitative and validated assay. Potency relative to an appropriate in-house reference preparation should be established. Ideally, a potency assay would be established that can ensure the consistency of lots with established clinical performance. Often, this takes the form of an in vitro expression system.
rules.exceptions: Early discussion with the NRA to reach consensus on appropriateness of the proposed method is recommended. Consensus also sought on composite measure (plasmid DNA quantity + % supercoiled) as potential non-bioassay approach.

rules.rule_text: Each lot of vaccine must be tested for sterility. A test for endotoxin must be conducted on each lot with appropriate specifications defined. Pyrogenicity may be determined using the monocyte activation test. The innocuity/abnormal toxicity/general safety test must not be required or requested.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.4.4.6 — Safety, including sterility and endotoxin testing
rules.verbatim_quote: Each lot of vaccine should be tested for sterility... Furthermore, a test for endotoxin should be conducted on each lot and appropriate specifications defined... The test known as the innocuity, abnormal toxicity or general safety test should not be required or requested (see section A.3.4.4 above).
rules.exceptions: If the vaccine is to be administered by a non-parenteral route, omission of the sterility test and inclusion of an appropriate alternative bioburden test needs to be appropriately justified.

rules.rule_text: For multi-component DNA vaccines, the development overview, control of production, and characterization of the bulk purified plasmid must be described for each plasmid. Careful consideration must be given to the control of the final formulated vaccine, including potency assessment.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.4.4.7 — Multi-component vaccines
rules.verbatim_quote: For each plasmid, the development overview, the control of production and the characterization of the bulk purified DNA vaccines that contain components (for example, immunomodulatory molecules or cytokine proteins) in addition to the plasmid(s), the role of the additional components should be addressed. Careful consideration must be given to the control of the final formulated vaccine.
rules.exceptions: If agreed to by the NRA, a composite measure of content (amount of plasmid DNA as used for dosing) and percentage of supercoiled plasmid may be used for control of potency of the lot.

rules.rule_text: The label on the carton or leaflet accompanying each container must include at minimum: vaccine name; manufacturer/distributor names and addresses; adjuvant nature and content; immunization schedule and recommended route(s) of administration; number of doses if multi-dose container; preservative name and concentration; antibiotics nature and quantity; storage temperature; expiry/retest date; special dosing schedules; and contraindications, warnings, precautions, and known adverse reactions.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.7 — Labelling
rules.verbatim_quote: The label on the carton enclosing one or more final containers, or the leaflet accompanying each container, should include the following information at a minimum: the name of the vaccine; the names and addresses of the manufacturer and distributer; a statement that specifies the nature and content of adjuvant contained in one human dose, if any; the immunization schedule, and the recommended route(s) of administration; the number of doses, if the product is issued in a multi-dose container; the name and concentration of any preservative added; a statement on the nature and quantity, or upper limit, of any antibiotics present in the vaccine; the temperature recommended during storage and transport; the expiry/retest date; any special dosing schedules; and contraindications, warnings and precautions, and information on concomitant vaccine use and on known adverse reactions.
rules.exceptions: None stated.
cross_references: WHO GMP for pharmaceutical products TRS 986 Annex 2
WHO GMP for biological products TRS 999 Annex 2

rules.rule_text: Distribution and transport must follow WHO GMP guidelines; shipments must be maintained within specified temperature ranges and packages must contain cold-chain monitors if temperature control is required. If no cold chain is claimed, conditions and supporting data must be provided.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.8 — Distribution and transport
rules.verbatim_quote: Shipments should be maintained within specified temperature ranges, as applicable, and packages should contain cold-chain monitors if the temperature needs to be controlled (84). If it is claimed that a cold-chain is not required then the conditions under which stability has been established (for example, maximum temperature and maximum length of time at that temperature) should be described and data supporting these claims provided.
rules.exceptions: Cold chain not required if stability demonstrated at ambient conditions with supporting data.
cross_references: WHO Model guidance for storage and transport TRS 961 Annex 9

rules.rule_text: Stability testing of the final product must comply with WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines. Real-time stability studies must be performed. Stability-indicating parameters must include appearance, quantity, and percentage of supercoiled plasmid. Accelerated studies may provide complementary but not primary evidence.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.9.1 — Stability testing
rules.verbatim_quote: Adequate stability studies form an essential part of vaccine development. The stability of the final product in the container proposed for use should, therefore, be determined and the results used to establish a shelf-life under appropriate storage conditions... Real-time stability studies should be undertaken for this purpose but accelerated stability studies at elevated temperatures may provide complementary supporting evidence... The stability assessment should comply with the WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines (82).
rules.exceptions: Container-closure system compatibility with storage stability should be assessed including leachables and extractables.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines TRS 962 Annex 3
WHO Guidelines on stability of vaccines for ECTC TRS 999 Annex 5

rules.rule_text: The expiry date must be defined on the basis of shelf-life in the final container and supported by stability studies approved by the NRA. The expiry date must be based on date of blending of final bulk, date of filling, or date of first valid potency test on the final lot, as appropriate, and agreed with the NRA.
rules.source_section: Part A, Section A.9.3 — Expiry date
rules.verbatim_quote: The expiry date should be defined on the basis of shelf-life in the final container and should be supported by stability studies approved by the NRA. The expiry date should be based on the date of blending of the final bulk, the date of filling or the date of the first valid potency test on the final lot, as appropriate, and agreed with the NRA.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: The nonclinical evaluation of the candidate DNA vaccine must be considered on a product-specific basis taking into account the intended clinical use of the product. The selection of appropriate studies must follow WHO guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccines and on nonclinical evaluation of vaccine adjuvants.
rules.source_section: Part B — Nonclinical evaluation of plasmid DNA vaccines
rules.verbatim_quote: The nonclinical evaluation of the candidate vaccine should be considered on a product-specific basis taking into account the intended clinical use of the product. The selection of appropriate studies relating to the toxicology and pharmacology (proof-of-concept) of the product may be determined based on either or both of the following WHO guidelines: WHO guidelines on nonclinical evaluation of vaccines (27); and WHO Guidelines on the nonclinical evaluation of vaccine adjuvants and adjuvanted vaccines (28).
rules.exceptions: For new DNA vaccines based on existing plasmid backbones with significant nonclinical and clinical experience, an abbreviated nonclinical programme should be considered.
cross_references: WHO TRS 927 Annex 1 — nonclinical evaluation of vaccines
WHO TRS 987 Annex 2 — nonclinical evaluation of vaccine adjuvants

rules.rule_text: Biodistribution studies are no longer required for plasmid DNA vaccines, as data to date have not shown reason to continue such evaluations; plasmid DNA remains largely at the injection site and does not biodistribute at clinically relevant levels or widely throughout the body.
rules.source_section: Part B — Nonclinical evaluation of plasmid DNA vaccines
rules.verbatim_quote: Although biodistribution studies were previously suggested for DNA vaccines, the data acquired to date have not shown reason to continue with such evaluations. Plasmid DNA remains largely at the injection site and does not biodistribute at clinically relevant levels or widely throughout the body.
rules.exceptions: A limited amount of information is available from developmental toxicology and biodistribution studies in maternal or fetal animals; publication of any developmental toxicology studies already performed is encouraged.

rules.rule_text: Clinical studies of plasmid DNA vaccines must adhere to WHO Guidelines for good clinical practice (GCP) for trials on pharmaceutical products and the WHO Guidelines on clinical evaluation of vaccines: regulatory expectations.
rules.source_section: Part C — Clinical evaluation of plasmid DNA vaccines
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical studies should adhere to the principles described in the WHO Guidelines for good clinical practice (GCP) for trials on pharmaceutical products (91) and the WHO Guidelines on clinical evaluation of vaccines: regulatory expectations (29).
rules.exceptions: None stated.
cross_references: WHO TRS 1004 Annex 9 — clinical evaluation of vaccines

rules.rule_text: Where a specific delivery device has been used to demonstrate efficacy, the labelling must reflect the device parameters used in the pivotal trial(s), and must make clear that only the authorized device must be used. Pharmacovigilance plans must capture any off-label use of alternative devices.
rules.source_section: Part C — Clinical evaluation of plasmid DNA vaccines
rules.verbatim_quote: When a specific delivery device has been used to demonstrate the efficacy of a DNA vaccine, the labelling should reflect the device parameters used in the pivotal trial(s), as should the protocols for those trials. The labelling should make it clear to the user that only the authorized device must be used. It will be important to consider how pharmacovigilance plans will capture any off-label use of an alternative device, including needle and syringe delivery.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Pivotal efficacy trials for plasmid DNA vaccines should use a double-blinded randomized placebo-controlled trial design whenever feasible.
rules.source_section: Part C — Clinical evaluation of plasmid DNA vaccines
rules.verbatim_quote: It is important to maintain a double-blinded randomized placebo-controlled trial design to obtain pivotal efficacy data, whenever feasible.
rules.exceptions: The appropriateness of using the delivery device with a substance other than the intended candidate vaccine has to be considered in terms of ethics and risk-benefit considerations.

rules.rule_text: No new biological product (including plasmid DNA vaccines) should be released until consistency of lot manufacturing and quality has been established and demonstrated by the manufacturer. A vaccine lot should be released only if it fulfils all national requirements and/or satisfies Part A of these WHO Guidelines or disease-specific WHO guidelines.
rules.source_section: Part D — Guidelines for NRAs, Sections D.1 and D.2
rules.verbatim_quote: These guidelines specify that no new biological product should be released until consistency of lot manufacturing and quality has been established and demonstrated by the manufacturer... A vaccine lot should be released only if it fulfils all national requirements and/or satisfies Part A of these WHO Guidelines or disease-specific WHO guidelines, as relevant to the product.
rules.exceptions: The guidelines do not apply to material for clinical trials.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines for national authorities on quality assurance for biological products TRS 822 Annex 2
WHO Guidelines for independent lot release of vaccines TRS 978 Annex 2
